title: String Slicing
content:
### String Slicing

**Slicing** extracts a portion of a string.

---

### Basic Syntax

```python
string[start:end:step]
```

- `start` -- starting index (inclusive, default 0)
- `end` -- ending index (exclusive, default end)
- `step` -- step size (default 1)

---

### Examples

```python
text = "Hello, World!"

print(text[0:5])    # "Hello" (indices 0 to 4)
print(text[7:12])   # "World"
print(text[:5])     # "Hello" (start default 0)
print(text[7:])     # "World!" (goes to end)
```

### Negative Indexing

```python
text = "Hello, World!"

print(text[-6:-1])  # "World"
print(text[-5:])    # "orld!"
```

---

### Step

```python
text = "Hello, World!"

print(text[::2])    # "Hlo ol!" (every 2nd char)
print(text[::-1])   # "!dlroW ,olleH" (reverse!)
```

---

### Slicing Rules

| Slice | Result | Explanation |
|-------|--------|-------------|
| `s[0:5]` | `Hello` | First 5 characters |
| `s[6:]` | `World!` | From index 6 to end |
| `s[-5:]` | `orld!` | Last 5 characters |
| `s[::-1]` | Reversed | Reverse the string |
| `s[::2]` | Alternating | Every 2nd character |

---

> **Key Point:** Slice indices are `[start, end)` -- includes start, excludes end. Think of the end index as "up to but not including".
codeExample:
text = "Hello, World!"

print("Full:", text)
print("First 5:", text[:5])
print("World:", text[7:12])
print("Reversed:", text[::-1])
print("Every 2nd:", text[::2])
print("Last 3:", text[-3:])
